hydross.SCORECARD_FINDING: 17th percentile; overheal (73.5%) exceeded the Top 100 sample's worst real parse (59.5%) by 14 points, and HPM (2.47) ran at just 0.42x the sample average - together the clearest story of this kill, more than any single spell or cooldown choice. Active time (98.7%) was in line with the sample.
hydross.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Composition itself was close to the sample's own mix (Lifebloom 40.4% vs. 41.1%, Regrowth 40.1% vs. 27.6%) - the real gap this kill is in how much of that healing landed as overheal, not what was cast.
hydross.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Nature's Swiftness went unused against an 84% sample usage rate. Swiftmend was cast once (on Thugalina) against a sample average of 3.8 casts - a real, if not individually flagged, undercast. One death (Hezrun) trailed the single Innervate cast by 18 seconds, too far apart to read as a missed save.
hydross.TARGET_FINDING: Target coverage (97.9%) ran above the sample average (83.5%) - almost all healing landed on the top 5 recipients - though the top target (Captinhelp, 35.8%) took a smaller share than the sample's typical top-target concentration (48.5%).

lurker.SCORECARD_FINDING: 19th percentile; overheal (65.2%) again exceeded the Top 100 sample's worst parse (49.1%) by 16 points, continuing the same pattern as Hydross. HPM (3.89) ran at 0.53x the sample average. Active time (96.8%) stayed in line.
lurker.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Swiftmend (19.3%) ran well above the sample's 4.7% share - the largest single spell gap of the kill - while Regrowth (28.9%) undershot the sample's 39.2%.
lurker.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Innervate went unused against a 90% sample usage rate, and Mana Potion also went unused (59% sample rate). Swiftmend was cast 5 times, above the sample's own average of 3.2, spread across five different targets - none of them the one death this kill (Staubaoz), so the 7-second gap between that death and the nearest Swiftmend cast reads as coincidental rather than a near-miss save.
lurker.TARGET_FINDING: Target coverage (56.9%) matched the sample almost exactly (56.8%), though the top target (Vajony, 15.0%) took a smaller share than the sample's typical concentration (25.3%) - healing was spread wider than usual at the top end.

leotheras.SCORECARD_FINDING: 26th percentile; overheal (69.3%) exceeded the sample's worst parse (54.3%) by 15 points, continuing the raid-wide pattern - the third straight kill with this exact deviation. HPM (3.24) ran at 0.51x the sample average. Target coverage (63.0%) ran above the sample average (51.4%), and active time (99.6%) was in line.
leotheras.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Regrowth (36.3%) undershot the sample's 52.8% share - the opposite direction from the last two kills, where Vinnyvozz's spells ran ahead of the sample rather than behind. Rejuvenation (21.8%) ran ahead of the sample's 13.3% to compensate.
leotheras.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Nature's Swiftness went unused against an 81% sample usage rate. Tranquility was cast once (11.8% of healing), against just a 6% Top 100 usage rate on this boss. Five deaths this kill; the closest (Calamety, 2.9 seconds after a Swiftmend cast) doesn't line up with a real save opportunity, since that Swiftmend was aimed at Silkyrebel, Lasmur, and self, not Calamety.
leotheras.TARGET_FINDING: Target coverage (63.0%) ran above the sample average (51.4%); the top target (Thugalina, 23.2%) took a somewhat larger share than the sample's typical 15.6%, though still in line overall.

karathress.SCORECARD_FINDING: 26th percentile, tied with Leotheras; overheal (59.1%) exceeded the sample's worst parse (46.5%) by 13 points - the fourth straight kill with this pattern - and HPM (4.28) ran at 0.52x the sample average. Active time (99.7%) was in line, and no deaths this kill.
karathress.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Rejuvenation (24.1%) ran well ahead of the sample's 12.9% share, the largest gap this kill; Lifebloom (30.6%) undershot the sample's 40.0%.
karathress.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Nature's Swiftness again went unused against a 74% sample usage rate. Everything else - Innervate, Swiftmend (3 casts, close to the sample's own 2.9 average), Mana Potion - landed in line with the sample. No deaths this kill, so no cooldown-timing question to check.
karathress.TARGET_FINDING: Thugalina again took the largest single share of healing (47.1%), well above the sample's typical top-target concentration (34.8%) - a real, tank-heavy kill, not the wider spread seen on most other bosses this raid.

morogrim.SCORECARD_FINDING: 32nd percentile, the best relative kill of the raid so far; overheal (66.9%) still exceeded the sample's worst parse (51.1%) by 16 points, and HPM (3.54) ran at 0.5x the sample average - the same pattern as every other kill, just at the raid's strongest percentile.
morogrim.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Lifebloom (24.6%) undershot the sample's 37.4% share - the widest gap this kill - while Swiftmend (13.8%) ran well ahead of the sample's 5.5%.
morogrim.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Nature's Swiftness went unused against an 87% sample usage rate. Swiftmend was cast 7 times (Hezrun, Dormammoo, Lasmur, Leechers twice, Bronimo, self), above the sample's own average of 5.1. Tranquility was cast once (5.7% of healing), well below the 33% Top 100 usage rate on this boss. No deaths this kill.
morogrim.TARGET_FINDING: Captinhelp took the largest healing share (20.5%), well below the sample's typical top-target concentration (38.8%) - the widest, most spread-out healing pattern of the raid so far.

vashj.SCORECARD_FINDING: 30th percentile; overheal (67.2%) exceeded the sample's worst parse (53.5%) by 14 points, and HPM (3.96) ran at 0.57x the sample average - the same raid-wide pattern. Active time (98.9%), target coverage, and top-target concentration all landed in line with the sample.
vashj.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Swiftmend (14.5%) ran well ahead of the sample's 4.4% share, the largest gap this kill; Regrowth (14.7%) undershot the sample's 21.9%.
vashj.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Nature's Swiftness went unused against an 86% sample usage rate. Swiftmend was cast 10 times across nine different targets, well above the sample's own average of 6.9. Four deaths this kill; the closest (Calamety, 1.6 seconds after an Innervate cast) is close in time, but Innervate restores mana rather than preventing death, so it isn't a real missed-save correlation - the other three deaths trail Swiftmend casts by 9.6-31.5 seconds, too far to read as related.
vashj.TARGET_FINDING: Target coverage (45.0%) and top-target concentration (15.6%) both landed in line with the sample - the one kill this raid where target spread wasn't itself a notable deviation.

alar.SCORECARD_FINDING: The lowest percentile of the raid (7th); overheal (77.7%) exceeded the sample's worst parse (61.1%) by nearly 17 points - the widest gap of any kill so far - and HPM (2.57) ran at just 0.41x the sample average, also among the lowest of the raid. Active time (99.9%) was in line.
alar.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Regrowth (7.5%) undershot the sample's 20.5% share by the widest margin of any kill this raid; Lifebloom (58.0%) ran ahead of the sample's 49.9%.
alar.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Nature's Swiftness went unused against an 85% sample usage rate. Swiftmend was cast 8 times across eight targets, above the sample's own average of 6.3 - none of them the one death this kill (Leechers), and the nearest Swiftmend trails that death by 20 seconds, too far to read as related.
alar.TARGET_FINDING: Target coverage (72.3%) and top-target concentration (40.2%, Thugalina) both landed in line with the sample - target spread wasn't the issue on the raid's weakest kill; HPS and overheal were.

voidreaver.SCORECARD_FINDING: 32nd percentile, tied for the raid's best; overheal (51.2%) exceeded the sample's worst parse (33.0%) by 18 points, and HPM (6.03, 0.72x the sample average) was the closest to sample-normal of any kill this raid, even though this was the one kill with no flask or elixir active at all (per the gear audit below). Target coverage (56.5%) ran well above the sample average (37.2%).
voidreaver.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The widest spell-composition gap of the raid: Regrowth covered just 25.7% of healing against a 76.4% sample share, while Lifebloom (45.3%) ran far ahead of the sample's 4.9%.
voidreaver.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Nature's Swiftness went unused against the lowest sample-usage rate of any kill this raid (54%) but still crossed this pipeline's undercast threshold. Everything else - Innervate, Swiftmend, Mana Potion - landed in line or close to it. No deaths this kill.
voidreaver.TARGET_FINDING: The one kill this raid where Vinnyvozz's own name led the target list (13.7% self-healing), with coverage (56.5%) well above the sample average (37.2%) - a real, wide-spread kill rather than a concentrated one.

solarian.SCORECARD_FINDING: The second-lowest percentile of the raid (10th) and the widest overheal gap of any kill (87.9% vs. a sample worst of 65.4%, +22.5 points) - also the lowest HPM (1.86, 0.38x the sample average) and lowest HPS-to-average ratio (0.23x) of the raid. Active time (95.9%) stayed in line despite the rest.
solarian.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Regrowth was never cast this kill at all, versus a 59.2% share in the Top 100 sample - the single largest composition gap of the raid. Lifebloom (76.1%) covered the vast majority of healing instead, far ahead of the sample's 13.2%.
solarian.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Three cooldowns went unused against real sample usage: Innervate (82% sample rate), Nature's Swiftness (72%), and Mana Potion (60%) - the most cooldowns flagged as missing on any single kill this raid. No deaths this kill.
solarian.TARGET_FINDING: Target coverage (58.5%) and top-target concentration (18.1%, Banepain) both landed in line with the sample - the low HPS/HPM/overheal numbers above are the real story on this kill, not target spread.

kaelthas.SCORECARD_FINDING: 24th percentile; overheal (71.7%) exceeded the sample's worst parse (61.7%) by 10 points - the smallest gap of the raid, though still a real one - and HPM (3.58) ran at 0.59x the sample average. By far the deadliest kill of the raid at 16 deaths, more than triple the next-highest (Leotheras, 5).
kaelthas.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Regrowth (11.9%) undershot the sample's 33.5% share; Lifebloom (51.4%) ran ahead of the sample's 39.4% to compensate.
kaelthas.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Rebirth went unused despite a real 62% sample usage rate on this specific boss - the only kill this raid where Rebirth crossed this pipeline's undercast threshold, and notably the one with by far the most deaths (16) to potentially answer. Swiftmend was cast 9 times, in line with the sample's own average (8.3); one of those casts targeted Enëlye, who nonetheless appears twice in this kill's death list - a real, checkable case worth a closer look rather than the coincidental timing seen on most other kills. The other 15 deaths trail the nearest Swiftmend cast by 1.6-19.8 seconds but weren't that cast's actual target.
kaelthas.TARGET_FINDING: Target coverage (56.7%) and top-target concentration (18.9%, Captinhelp) both landed in line with the sample.

RaidOverview.GEAR_CONSISTENCY_FINDING: not fully consistent - Main Hand differs across kills and is flagged for review, alongside one single-kill Ranged swap
RaidOverview.GEAR_FINDING_NOTE: The Main Hand variant doesn't read as a clean single-kill swap the way Ranged does - worth confirming whether this was a deliberate weapon change mid-raid or a real equipping gap on some kills. Back (item 29989) carries no permanent enchant. Consumables were complete on 9 of 10 kills; Void Reaver is the one exception, with no flask or elixir of either kind active that pull.
RaidOverview.RAID_SUMMARY_FINDING: The dominant story of this raid is overheal: it exceeded the Top 100 sample's worst real parse on all 10 kills, ranging from 10 points past the worst (Kael'thas) to 22.5 points past it (Solarian), paired with HPM running 0.38x-0.72x the sample average on every kill - a consistent, raid-wide pattern rather than a per-kill outlier. Nature's Swiftness went almost entirely unused (cast on only 2 of 10 kills, a real deviation from an 81-98% sample usage rate on most of the other bosses). Rebirth was never cast the entire raid despite 6 of 10 kills having at least one death, most notably Kael'thas Sunstrider's 16 deaths (more than triple any other kill) against a real 62% Top 100 usage rate on that specific boss - the only kill where that gap crossed this pipeline's real threshold. The three weakest kills by percentile were Al'ar (7th), Solarian (10th), and Hydross the Unstable (17th). One real gear note: the Back slot carries no permanent enchant, and Main Hand varies across kills in a way that needs a closer look rather than reading as a clean single-kill swap.
RaidOverview.RAID_WARNING_BANNER: Overheal exceeded the Top 100 sample's worst real parse on every single kill this raid (10.0 to 22.5 points past the sample's own worst), paired with HPM running 0.38x-0.72x the sample average on all 10 kills - the most consistent raid-wide pattern of any healer analyzed so far, and the clearest explanation for the below-average HPS on every kill.
